_about: Georgian water-sector legal instruments. Canonical source is matsne.gov.ge (Legislative Herald of Georgia, the official gazette); FAOLEX (fao.org/faolex) is cited where the instrument is indexed there. Local copies (matsne HTML/PDF, FAOLEX PDFs) are gitignored; this manifest is the audit trail. 'status' / 'last_verified' reflect that the URL was confirmed to resolve (HTTP 200) during the build run. FAOLEX detail pages and matsne document pages are JavaScript-rendered, so article text was cross-checked via the research pass, not scraped. Items whose article numbers or record ids could NOT be confirmed against a primary rendered page are marked pending and are deliberately NOT asserted as precise citations in the dataset.
_water_corpus: On 2026-06-01 the in-force FAOLEX water-area corpus for Georgia was enumerated directly from the FAOLEX query endpoint (database=faolex; AREA:WA AND CC:GEO; REPEALED:N AND SUPERS:N; laws, regulations and misc, excluding policy), which returned 59 records each carrying FaolexId, Title_of_Text and Date_of_Text. Every LEX-FAOC id added in this run was returned by that endpoint, so the ids and the existence of the instruments are primary-verified against FAOLEX itself; the FAOLEX detail pages and matsne pages remain JavaScript-rendered, so pinpoint article numbers are still NOT asserted unless separately confirmed. The enumeration surfaced no in-force transboundary water treaty (no Kura or Enguri basin agreement) in the FAOLEX water-area corpus.
_targets_and_financing: Sector targets and the financing gap are not set by a single Georgian law, so they are sourced to OECD assessments (secondary) and surfaced as key_insights, not as instruments. Verified by reading the source PDFs on 2026-06-02: (1) OECD water-policy outlook for Georgia, Moldova and Ukraine (2021), Table 2.11, full-reform scenario: present value of additional financial requirements GEL 793.4 million (EUR 198.9 million), of which wastewater and water-supply projects GEL 784.0 million (EUR 196.5 million), horizon to 2030, largely donor-dependent (https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2021/07/developing-a-water-policy-outlook-for-georgia-the-republic-of-moldova-and-ukraine_a1b2f035/512a52aa-en.pdf). (2) OECD 10th National Policy Dialogue overview, Georgia, Jan 2024: the only firm sanitation milestone is the EU Urban Waste Water Directive approximation target 'at least ten urban WWTPs in major settlements', which slipped from 2021 to 2025 to 2026, and the household water-metering target is left 'to be determined' (https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/about/programmes/water-policy-reforms-in-eecca/georgia/overview_10th_georgia_npd_jan_2024.pdf). The nearest thing to a non-revenue-water performance norm is GNERC's normative drinking-water-loss rule (Resolution No. 45 of 2017, LEX-FAOC187451), already in the corpus, which caps the loss level recoverable in tariffs but is not an NRW reduction target. Negative findings: no quantified national target for water or sanitation access was located; Georgia is a Party to the UNECE-WHO Protocol on Water and Health but does not appear to have adopted Article-6 national targets, a point drawn from secondary summaries and NOT confirmed against the (access-blocked) primary UNECE reports, so it is flagged, not asserted. Caution: the '2014-2030 WSS Strategy' and 'entire population by 2028' figures elsewhere in the 2021 OECD report belong to Moldova, not Georgia, and were excluded.

_revisions.location: data/georgia.ts (wss_urban policy cell); data/georgia-insights.ts (new lead insight); intro
_revisions.old_text: No consolidated national urban-WSS policy / Georgia has no national WSS policy.
_revisions.new_text: A national WSS policy exists: the Water Supply and Sanitation Vision and Policy Statement (MRDI, November 2021). Nuance preserved: it is a non-binding vision/policy statement (unofficial English translation), not a binding WSS framework law.

_revisions.location: data/georgia.ts (wss_urban + wss_rural regulation cells); data/georgia-insights.ts (drinking-water insight); manifest drinking-water entry
_revisions.old_text: No drinking-water-quality technical regulation has been adopted; revised standards reportedly slipping to 2030 (treated as a regulatory gap).
_revisions.new_text: A drinking-water-quality standard has been in force since 2014 (Government Decree N58, 15 Jan 2014). What remains pending is the EU-aligned revision (~2030).
_revisions.source: Government of Georgia Decree N58 of 15 Jan 2014, https://matsne.gov.ge/ka/document/view/2196792; local primary copy 'Drinking-water technical regulation 2014 (GEO).pdf' in the 2026-06-10 OneDrive drop

_revisions.location: data/georgia.ts (wss_urban headline + institutions cell); data/georgia-insights.ts (urban-split insight)
_revisions.old_text: Georgian Water and Power (GWP) serves Tbilisi, Mtskheta and Rustavi.
_revisions.new_text: GWP serves Tbilisi and Mtskheta; Rustavi is served by the separately-licensed Rustavi Water. This also REVERSES the implementation plan's own §4.4 'no change' decision, which had relied on a secondary roster; the primary MRDI roster lists Rustavi Water as a distinct licensed utility.
_revisions.source: WSS Vision & Policy Statement (MRDI, Nov 2021), Appendix 2, Table 1 (licensed-utility roster)

_revisions.old_text: water_access: qualitative aim only / no quantified access target; sanitation: No quantified national target (not_set).
_revisions.new_text: Both rows now carry the 2030 WSS Vision goal (universal safe water; adequate sanitation) against the GEOSTAT-2020 baseline (77.6% / 79.2%), status 'set', with the explicit caveat that the baseline is a one-off 2020 survey and no quantified intermediate target exists.
_revisions.source: WSS Vision & Policy Statement (MRDI, Nov 2021), Vision section + para 50, fn 7 (GEOSTAT-2020 baseline)

_misnamed_source_files.actual_content: Order No. 36 of 18 March 2005 of the Minister of Environment and Natural Resources approving a regulation on the conservation of agricultural and other lands degraded by toxic production waste and radioactive contamination (a contaminated-land conservation decree under the 2003 Law on Soil Conservation), NOT the Law on Land Amelioration.
_misnamed_source_files.note: The manifest's 'Law on Land Amelioration' instrument entry correctly cites the FAO-hosted geo54807.pdf (the real Amelioration Law); this mislabeled local .doc is NOT used as that instrument's source. The Amelioration Law full text is therefore NOT present in the 2026-06-10 drop, so its citations remain named-in-words.

_misnamed_source_files.actual_content: Rural Development Strategy of Georgia for 2017-2020 / Action Plan for 2017 (ENPARD, published with EU / UNDP / FAO support), NOT the Irrigation Strategy 2017-2025.
_misnamed_source_files.note: The manifest's 'Irrigation Strategy 2017-2025' instrument entry correctly cites the FAO-hosted geo171443.pdf; this mislabeled local PDF is NOT used as that instrument's source.

instruments.full_title: Constitution of Georgia (consolidated)
instruments.note: Environmental and natural-resource provisions. matsne is canonical; constitutions are not FAOLEX-indexed.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Water (No. 936)
instruments.note: Registration No. 936, adopted 16 Oct 1997. In force but sunsetting: the matsne record shows expiration 1 Sept 2026, when the 2023 Water Resources Management Law takes over. No FAOLEX listing retrieved. State ownership of water bodies; general vs special water use; sanitary protection zones.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Water Resources Management
instruments.note: Adopted 30 June 2023. Transposes the EU Water Framework Directive: seven river basins grouped into river-basin management districts with management plans; restores the special water-use / abstraction-permit regime (abolished 2008); environmental-flow protection; consultative basin councils under MEPA. Operative norms bind across the territory from 1 Sept 2026; repeals the 1997 Law on Water. FAOLEX URL resolves (HTTP 200); detail page is JS-rendered. The matsne document-view id and pinpoint article numbers were NOT confirmed against the primary text and are not asserted as precise article citations in the dataset.

instruments.short: Law on Energy and Water Supply (2019)
instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Energy and Water Supply (No. 5646-rs)
instruments.note: GNERC enabling law. Registration No. 5646-rs, adopted 20 Dec 2019. Extends GNERC's economic regulation to water supply (Art. 1(9)); defines water-supply licensing terms and licence requirement (Arts. 3, 11). FAOLEX id corroborated via FAOLEX / Ecolex / UNEP-LEAP listings; both matsne and FAOLEX URLs resolve (HTTP 200). The specific tariff-setting article number was not confirmed against a rendered page and is not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Water Users Organisations (No. 5609-rs)
instruments.note: Registration No. 5609-rs, adopted 19 Dec 2019. Enables membership-based water-user organisations as legal entities of public law (Art. 6), founded by users of >50% of a service area (Art. 7); defines irrigation infrastructure (Art. 3). matsne URL resolves (HTTP 200). FAOLEX listing LEX-FAOC193150 confirmed via the FAOLEX water-area corpus; the prior run's 'no FAOLEX listing retrieved' note is superseded.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Public Safety (No. 2467-IIs)
instruments.note: Registration No. 2467-IIs, adopted 29 May 2014. Civil-protection / emergency framework; the Emergency Management Agency (under the Ministry of Internal Affairs) runs the unified system. Emergency prevention via forecasting and monitoring (Art. 28); population warning (Art. 32). matsne URL resolves (HTTP 200). No FAOLEX listing retrieved.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Land Amelioration
instruments.note: Assigns state management/control of land amelioration (irrigation and drainage) to the agriculture ministry; addresses water-consumption limits and filtration losses. FAO-hosted PDF (geo54807) retrieved and resolves (HTTP 200); the FAOLEX LEX-FAOC record id and a matsne document-view id were not resolved. Year approximate (the text carries a 07.03.2003 amendment marker).

instruments.full_title: Irrigation Strategy for Georgia 2017-2025
instruments.note: Documents the post-2012 recentralisation of irrigation under Georgian Amelioration LLC as a single main-system provider. FAO-hosted PDF (geo171443) resolves (HTTP 200); LEX-FAOC id not resolved. Secondary policy instrument.

instruments.full_title: Georgia's 2030 Climate Change Strategy and 2021-2023 Action Plan
instruments.note: Adaptation priorities include agriculture and water. FAO-hosted PDF (geo209870) resolves (HTTP 200); also published by MEPA and climate-laws.org. Secondary policy instrument.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 21 of 2017 of GNERC on the Methodology of Calculating Water Supply Tariffs
instruments.note: The 'Resolution N21 of 2017' lead flagged unconfirmed in the prior run is now confirmed via the FAOLEX water-area corpus (LEX-FAOC187453): the methodology for calculating drinking-water-supply tariffs, the basis for per-licensee tariffs (GWP, UWSCG, Adjara operators). From 1 Apr 2026 GNERC also sets irrigation tariffs (set 29 Dec 2025). gnerc.org pages time out / 403 to automated fetches; the FAOLEX id is returned by the query endpoint itself, but the detail page is JS-rendered so pinpoint article numbers are not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 45 of 2017 of GNERC on Calculating Normative Losses of Potable Water
instruments.note: Rule for calculating the normative (allowed) losses of potable water, an input to the water-supply tariff methodology. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 2 of 2011 of GNERC on Tariffs for Amelioration Services
instruments.note: Standing tariff instrument for amelioration (irrigation and drainage) services, predating the revised irrigation tariffs effective 1 Apr 2026. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Subsoil (No. 242-IIS)
instruments.note: No. 242-IIS, adopted 17 May 1996. Treats underground water (potable, mineral, thermal, industrial, technical) as a licensed subsoil resource under the National Agency of Subsoil Resources; general and agricultural groundwater abstraction sits under the water-use regime, not the subsoil licence. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.short: Engineering Protection of Water Objects Law (2000)
instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on Regulation and Engineering Protection of Seas, Water Objects and River Coastlines (No. 576-IS)
instruments.note: No. 576-IS, adopted 27 Oct 2000. Governs engineering protection of river coastlines, water objects and the sea coast against flooding and bank erosion; the standing legal basis for flood-defence and bank-protection works. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 137 of 2005 of the Government of Georgia on Rules for Issuance of Permits for Extracting Water from and Releasing Water into Surface Water Objects
instruments.note: Legacy rules for permits to extract water from, and release water into, surface water objects; predates the 2008 deregulation of special water use and the 2023 restored permit regime. Returned as non-repealed in the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 414 of 2013 of the Government of Georgia on Calculation of Permitted Limits of Contaminants in Waters Flowing into Surface Water Objects
instruments.note: Method for calculating permitted limits of contaminants in waters discharged to surface water objects. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 425 of 2013 of the Government of Georgia on Protection of Surface Waters of Georgia from Pollution
instruments.note: Measures for protecting Georgia's surface waters from pollution. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 440 of 2013 of the Government of Georgia on Water Protection Zones
instruments.note: Establishes water-protection zones along water bodies. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 409 of 2013 of the Government of Georgia on Technical Use of Amelioration Systems
instruments.note: Rules for the technical use of amelioration (irrigation and drainage) systems. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 42 of 2017 of the Minister of Environment and Natural Resources on Guidelines for Classification and Drafting Reports for Assessment of Reserves and Predicted Resources of Underground Water
instruments.note: Classifies and assesses reserves of potable, mineral, industrial, technical and thermal underground water; the basis for groundwater-reserve accounting. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 161 of 2019 of the Government of Georgia on Rules for Determining and Approving Sanitary Protection Zones of Groundwater Objects under Mineral Extraction Licence
instruments.note: Sets sanitary protection zones around groundwater objects subject to a mineral-extraction licence. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Resolution No. 431 of 2018 of the Government of Georgia on Rules for Discharging and Receiving Water Flowing into the Sewerage System and Permitted Limits of Pollutants
instruments.note: Rules for discharging into and receiving water from the sewerage system, with permitted pollutant limits; the operative urban-wastewater discharge standard. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 2-686 of 2023 of the Minister of Environmental Protection and Agriculture on Rules for the Water-Supply Regime and Irrigation Regulations
instruments.note: Rules for the water-supply regime and irrigation regulations operated by Georgian Amelioration. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 2-268 of 2021 of the Minister of Environmental Protection and Agriculture on Terms of Irrigation Service Agreements with Primary Water Users and Water User Organisations
instruments.note: Terms of irrigation-service agreements between Georgian Amelioration and primary water users / Water Users Organisations. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 2-531 of 2020 of the Minister of Environmental Protection and Agriculture on the Template Charter for a Water User Organisation
instruments.note: Template charter for Water Users Organisations, an implementing order under the 2019 WUO Law. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 2-569 of 2020 of the Minister of Environmental Protection and Agriculture on Rules for Maintaining the Water User Organisations' Registry
instruments.note: Rules for maintaining the Water Users Organisations registry, an implementing order under the 2019 WUO Law. Id from the FAOLEX water-area corpus; article numbers not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Order No. 1-1/13 of the Minister of Economy of Georgia (11 Jan 2010) establishing the United Water Supply Company of Georgia
instruments.note: UWSCG (100% state-owned LLC, overseen by MRDI) registered 14 Jan 2010, merging 66 local water companies; serves urban-type settlements except Tbilisi, Mtskheta, Rustavi, Gardabani and Adjara. The establishing instrument is a Minister's Order (No. 1-1/13), not a matsne-published government ordinance; no matsne id located. mrdi.gov.ge timed out on automated fetch this run; institutional facts corroborated via OECD/MEPA, Devex and the agency listing.

instruments.full_title: Government of Georgia Decree No. 58 of 15 January 2014 approving the technical regulation on drinking water
instruments.note: CORRECTED (see _revisions): a national drinking-water-quality standard HAS been in force since 2014 (Government Decree N58, 15 Jan 2014), contrary to the prior 'not yet adopted' reading drawn from the 2024 OECD/MEPA NPD overview. What remains pending is the EU-aligned revision, reportedly slipping to ~2030. matsne id 2196792; the page is Georgian-only and JavaScript-rendered, so any paraphrase is an unofficial machine translation (translate-with-caveat) and pinpoint article numbers are not asserted. Local primary copy held in the 2026-06-10 OneDrive drop. The unconfirmed FAOLEX 'Drinking Water Regulation' lead (LEX-FAOC227479) is still not asserted.

instruments.full_title: Association Agreement between the European Union and Georgia
instruments.note: Secondary policy driver: the environmental annex commits Georgia to approximate the EU Water Framework Directive and Drinking Water Directive, the stated driver for the 2023 Water Resources Management Law. EUR-Lex (CELEX 22014A0830(02)) is canonical and resolves (HTTP 200).

instruments.full_title: Water Supply and Sanitation Vision and Policy Statement of Georgia (MRDI, November 2021)
instruments.note: NEW (anchor for the 2026-06-10 corrections, see _revisions): the national WSS policy that the prior dataset reported as absent. Published by MRDI as an unofficial English translation; the moi.gov.ge PDF resolves (HTTP 200, ~732KB) and was read directly. Carries the GEOSTAT-2020 access baseline (77.6% water / 79.2% sanitation, para 50 + fn 7), the licensed-utility roster (Appendix 2, Table 1, used to correct the Rustavi attribution), and 2030 universal-access goals with a 24/7-supply target (cities by end-2025, rural by end-2030). A policy/vision statement, NOT a binding WSS framework law; pinpoint paragraph numbers cited are read from the English translation PDF.

instruments.full_title: Law of Georgia on the Development of High Mountainous Regions (No. 4036-RS, 16 July 2015)
instruments.local_file: 2024-2030 STRATEGY FOR DEVELOPMENT OF HIGH MOUNTAIN SETTLEMENTS OF GEORGIA.pdf
instruments.note: NEW: framework law (No. 4036-RS) underpinning the state obligation to develop high-mountain settlements, including basic infrastructure such as water supply; referenced from the rural-WSS policy cell. The cited instrument is the 2015 LAW (matsne /2924386, English, resolves HTTP 200). NOTE the local_file is the 2024-2030 implementing STRATEGY (a secondary document), not the law itself; per the plan we cite the verifiable law, not the strategy. Not FAOLEX-indexed.
